title: Traffic-related air pollution and respiratory symptoms in children living along trunk roads in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
ehr_evidence: None; the study used surveys of respiratory symptoms and ambient air monitoring stations.
summary: A prospective cohort study investigating the association between traffic-related air pollution (NO2 and SPM) and respiratory symptoms (asthma) in children living near trunk roads in Chiba Prefecture, Japan.
key_findings: Asthma prevalence was higher among girls living less than 50 m from trunk roads.
Asthma prevalence in girls increased significantly with higher air pollution concentrations.
Incidence of asthma in boys living in roadside areas significantly increased (OR = 3.75).
Incidence of asthma in girls also increased (OR = 4.06), but the risk was not statistically significant.
population: 3,234 children (aged 6-9 years) in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
limitations: Not explicitly stated in the provided text
